name: La'ie Falls Trail
tagline: A muddy, jungly adventure to a secluded North Shore waterfall most visitors never hear about.
parking: Limited parking near the trailhead off Poohaili Street in La'ie — respect the residential neighborhood
bestTime: After light rain for waterfall flow, but not during or right after heavy rain
talkStory: About 5 miles round trip with 800 ft elevation gain through thick tropical vegetation. The trail follows an old irrigation ditch route and crosses multiple streams. The falls at the end are about 25 feet and drop into a decent pool. This trail is perpetually muddy — no getting around it. Wear shoes you're okay with destroying and bring mosquito repellent. The trail can be hard to follow in spots, so pay attention to trail markers. Check current access status before going — the trailhead passes near private land and access has been restricted at times.
